title: Wet/Dry Mixture-of-Experts With Crossover Logic
summary: The FIA dry-tyre rule stops mattering once intermediates or wets are used, and Pirelli notes that missing the slick/intermediate crossover by one lap can cost 5-6 seconds.
rationale: The FIA dry-tyre rule stops mattering once intermediates or wets are used, and Pirelli notes that missing the slick/intermediate crossover by one lap can cost 5-6 seconds. Wet or drying laps are therefore a different prediction regime from standard dry-stint wear management.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for dry rows (`SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD`) and wet-transition rows (`INTERMEDIATE/WET`, plus optional anomaly-gated drying laps using large `LapTime_Delta` or lap-time spikes). Blend them with a simple gate based on `Compound`, `LapTime_Delta`, `RaceProgress`, and degradation features; calibrate each expert separately.
expected_effect: Cleaner signals on rare but strategically distinct wet segments, with less interference between mandatory-compound logic and crossover logic.
risk: Wet samples may be sparse, and heuristic gating can misclassify borderline drying-track laps.
